72a07425-a3f7-4574-801c-db3a578cb233.name: Crust
72a07425-a3f7-4574-801c-db3a578cb233.description: Hard crust covering the surface. Crust can be smooth or rough in nature.

229e9c86-e215-4bfd-96e6-44176f4a0727.name: Slush
229e9c86-e215-4bfd-96e6-44176f4a0727.description: Water-rich and partially melted snow in sheltered conditions.

06e4088b-9cdc-4db9-b931-bcdabd4b0547.name: Ice
06e4088b-9cdc-4db9-b931-bcdabd4b0547.description: Hard and unbroken icy layer on the surface. Ice forms as a result of melt-freeze cycles.

e906c9c7-c96d-4d80-ab4c-d22972b1b334.name: Powder snow
e906c9c7-c96d-4d80-ab4c-d22972b1b334.description: Recently fallen, light, soft and slightly consolidated frost snow.

580607c0-2ce3-4195-9fde-4e0228b6ff95.name: Powder
580607c0-2ce3-4195-9fde-4e0228b6ff95.description: Recently fallen loose, feather-light and non-consolidated snow. Powder usually forms in calm weather and hard frost.

695b2d74-59d8-4610-b33e-2be4e34f1e9c.name: Wet fresh snow
695b2d74-59d8-4610-b33e-2be4e34f1e9c.description: Snow from which you can easily make a snowball. Wet snow forms when it rains near freezing or well in sheltered areas.

fe316828-8034-42cb-8a2b-87744df3b94d.name: Sastrugi
fe316828-8034-42cb-8a2b-87744df3b94d.description: Wind-caused snow ridges that are hard, icy and sharp-crested.

f03adbfa-c85c-4f66-b4f1-20846a718bdc.name: Wavy snow
f03adbfa-c85c-4f66-b4f1-20846a718bdc.description: Wind-shaped fresh snow area. Waves are soft and easily broken.

be781456-689e-4cff-989a-f06cbe54ae6e.name: Wind-packed snow
be781456-689e-4cff-989a-f06cbe54ae6e.description: Smooth, wind-layered and compacted slab or lens. Wind-packed snow can also accumulate without snowfall if wind moves snow from place to place. Wind-packed snow typically forms in sheltered areas.

12c8dbeb-d380-48b4-aa37-aeb76fe2e308.name: Thin crust
12c8dbeb-d380-48b4-aa37-aeb76fe2e308.description: Snow crust that breaks under the skier's weight. Below the crust, snow can be sinking in places.

d83558df-a1e0-4c28-8a9d-557888a5c528.name: Breakable crust
d83558df-a1e0-4c28-8a9d-557888a5c528.description: Intermittently bearing, unexpectedly breaking snow surface. The crust can be very thick if there is porous snow beneath it.

2a0a2ec7-9541-40fe-9590-df92bbc889d3.name: Bearing crust
2a0a2ec7-9541-40fe-9590-df92bbc889d3.description: Solid and bearing snow crust, often with a very hard and dense surface.

da1bdc36-89fe-4dee-a5be-29a6b2b3f4fb.name: Breakable ice
da1bdc36-89fe-4dee-a5be-29a6b2b3f4fb.description: Hard and breakable icy layer on the surface.

480c682b-78b3-4b26-9eb1-6491d7299a4c.name: Softening snow
480c682b-78b3-4b26-9eb1-6491d7299a4c.description: Snow that becomes wet or moist from warming or rain starting from the surface.

97227e4e-7b27-4d44-bbfc-35b18880ad2c.name: Saturated snow
97227e4e-7b27-4d44-bbfc-35b18880ad2c.description: Wet, throughly soaked and whipped cream-like snow.

Sidebar.title: Pallaksen Pöllöt
Sidebar.p1: The snow application produced by Pallas Pöllöjen provides information about the prevailing snow conditions in the area. The application is in active use from February to May. The fell area is divided into segments, within which the snow is typically very similar.
Sidebar.p2: The information in the application is the subjective observations of the guides and the conditions can change quickly. When using snow data, it is worth taking into account the time stamp of the observation, as well as what additional information has been given about the segment and what the weather data about the area says. In the fells, the traveler is responsible for applying the observations, and the application does not in any way reduce his own responsibility.
Sidebar.p3: More detailed snow information for each segment opens by clicking on it. You can find information about the weather for the last few days and the entire season behind the weather button.
Sidebar.p4: Please remember to share your location with someone, for example on Whatsapp. Let's take care of ourselves, the fell and its beauty.
